title: Bacterial Transformation Protocol
author: Laura Sánchez
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.6taheie

steps.instruction: Take competent cells out of -80°C and thaw on ice (approximately 20-30 mins).
Chill approximately 5 ng (2 μl) of DNA in a 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube. * Please note: if draw from MoClo protocol samples, add the whole 20 μl of DNA
Add 50 μl of competent cells to the DNA. Mix gently by pipetting up and down or flicking the tube 4–5 times to mix the cells and DNA. Do not vortex.
Place the mixture on ice for 30 minutes. Do not mix.
Heat shock at 42°C for 45 seconds. Do not mix.
Add 950 μl of room temperature LB or SOC media to the tube.

Place tube at 37°C for 60 minutes. Shake vigorously (250 rpm) or rotate. * Please note: For the duration and temperature of the heat shock step as well as for the media to be used during the recovery period, please follow the recommendations provided by the competent cells’ manufacturer.
Prepare selection plates: LB + ampicillin 100 μg/mL LB + kanamycin 100 μg/mL LB + spectinomycin 50 μg/mL

Spread 50–100 μl of the cells and ligation mixture onto the plates, and incubate O/N at 37°C. * Please note: Remember to add always a negative control plate (with medium without cells) to check contamination, and a positive control plate (same cells transformed with pUC) to check the efficiency of the process. * Tip: to ensure that all the positive results are plated, it is advisable to pellet the rest of the sample and resuspend ot in a very low volume (50-100 μl), and then plate it.
Check the samples for contamination. If clean, continue the protocol.

Choose three positive colonies of each plate. Pick one with the inoculating loop and draw a strain in a new plate. Do the same with the other two, using the same plate but leaving a separation between each strain. Incubate O/N at 37°C. * This will allow you to maintain and store in a separated plate the single clones chosen from each initial plate, and work with them.

Prepare a liquid inoculum in a 15 mL Falcon tube of each of the three strains: pick a colony with the inoculating loop, resuspend it in 5 mL LB + antibiotic, and incubate 37°C O/N. * Tip: if the chosen colonies are big enough, you may be able to do both steps 12 and 13 together. Selection of recombinants
Take a 1.5 mL sample of the Falcon tubes into a new eppendorf, and perform a miniprep.
Check the plasmids following the Plasmid Verification Protocol, and/or sequence them.
